name: Ohio State University Extension - Fulton County
description_short: Ohio State University Extension's Fulton County office provides free educational programs in agriculture, 4-H youth development, family consumer sciences, and community development to Fulton County residents.
description_long:
Ohio State University Extension - Fulton County is a publicly funded educational outreach program operated by The Ohio State University's College of Food, Agricultural, and Environmental Sciences (CFAES). Established to serve Fulton County residents, the office operates from the Robert Fulton Agriculture Center in Wauseon and functions as part of a statewide university extension system dedicated to applied research and community education.

The office offers free educational programs across four primary focus areas: 4-H Youth Development for young people, Agriculture and Natural Resources for farmers and landowners, Community Development for local economic and civic initiatives, and Family and Consumer Sciences including nutrition and food safety education. Recent initiatives include SNAP-Ed nutrition programming (which ended in November 2025), food preservation education through the North Central Food Safety Extension Network, and various county-specific agricultural programs. The organization also hosts local radio programs and maintains active social media channels for program promotion.

As a university extension office, the Fulton County program is distinguished by its non-profit, public institution status with no financial incentive to sell products or services. All programming is free to county residents. The office employs dedicated extension staff and can connect residents to research-backed information from Ohio State University. The physical office location and consistent hours (Monday-Friday, 8:30 a.m. - 4:30 p.m., closed noon-12:45 p.m.) provide accessible local contact points for questions and program enrollment.

While the Fulton County Extension office excels at providing free educational resources and youth development opportunities, the organization's consumer finance relevance is limited. The office focuses primarily on agriculture, food safety, and 4-H programming rather than credit counseling, financial literacy, or debt management. Though Family and Consumer Sciences may tangentially address household financial topics, the website provides no evidence of dedicated credit counseling, housing counseling certification (HUD-approved), or NFCC membership typical of free-help category providers.
pros: Completely free educational programs and services to Fulton County residents
Direct affiliation with Ohio State University ensures access to research-backed educational content
Multiple program areas address diverse community needs including youth, agriculture, and family services
Local physical office location with consistent business hours and direct phone contact (419-337-9210)
Active engagement through multiple social media channels and local radio programs for outreach
Established 4-H Youth Development programs with documented applications and ambassador opportunities
Food safety education including SNAP-Ed nutrition programming and preservation guidance
cons: No evidence of credit counseling, housing counseling certification, or HUD approval on website
Limited relevance to consumer finance topics—primarily focused on agriculture and food safety education
Noon closure (12:00 p.m. - 12:45 p.m.) may create accessibility issues for some working residents
SNAP-Ed nutrition programming ended November 12, 2025, reducing available nutrition education resources
Website does not indicate NFCC membership or specialized financial counseling credentials
best_for: Fulton County farmers and agricultural producers seeking free crop, soil, and land management education
Families with youth interested in 4-H Youth Development programs and experiential learning
Residents seeking free food preservation, food safety, and nutrition education resources
Community organizations and local governments seeking extension expertise for civic development initiatives
diagnosis: Ohio State University Extension - Fulton County is best for Fulton County residents seeking free educational resources in agriculture, food safety, youth development, and community initiatives through a university-backed public program. However, this organization has no demonstrated expertise or focus in consumer finance, credit counseling, or debt management, making it inappropriate for consumers seeking credit repair, financial counseling, or debt assistance services.
services: 4-H Youth Development programming and youth ambassador applications
Agriculture and Natural Resources education for farmers and landowners
Community Development initiatives and technical assistance
Food preservation and food safety education
Local radio programs in Fulton County
Social media education through Facebook and YouTube channels
Preserve It Fresh, Preserve It Safe food preservation newsletter
County news and updates on agricultural and community topics
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 8770 State Route 108, Suite A, Robert Fulton Agriculture Center, Wauseon, OH 43567
